events.title: April Fools' Day
events.summary: A day for playing practical jokes and spreading hoaxes. In the UK, tradition dictates that jokes must be played before midday; anyone attempting a prank after noon is considered the fool themselves.

events.title: National Pet Month (UK) Starts
events.summary: Running from 1 April to 3 May, this campaign promotes responsible pet ownership and raises funds for UK animal charities while celebrating the bond between humans and their animal companions.

events.title: Edible Book Day
events.summary: A light-hearted global event where bibliophiles create 'books' made of food. Creations are exhibited and then eaten, usually held on or around the birthday of French gastronome Jean-Anthelme Brillat-Savarin.

events.title: World Autism Awareness Day
events.summary: Designated by the United Nations to encourage member states to take measures to raise awareness about people with Autism Spectrum Disorder throughout the world.

events.title: International Children's Book Day
events.summary: Organised by the International Board on Books for Young People (IBBY) to inspire a love of reading and to call attention to children's literature, held on Hans Christian Andersen’s birthday.

events.title: Peanut Butter and Jelly Day
events.summary: A light-hearted day celebrating the classic sandwich combination. Whilst more popular in the US, it is often used by snack enthusiasts in the UK as an excuse for a nostalgic lunch.

events.title: World Health Day
events.summary: A global health awareness day celebrated under the sponsorship of the World Health Organization (WHO) to mark the founding of the organisation and highlight a priority area of global health.

events.title: International Day of Reflection on the 1994 Genocide against the Tutsi in Rwanda
events.summary: A UN observance to honour the victims of the genocide in Rwanda and to promote the prevention of future atrocities through education and remembrance.

events.title: No Housework Day
events.summary: A light-hearted day encouraging people to take a break from the endless cycle of cleaning, dusting, and laundry. The goal is to focus on relaxation or hobbies instead of domestic chores.

events.title: Earth Day
events.summary: An annual event to demonstrate support for environmental protection. First held in 1970, it now includes a wide range of events coordinated globally by Earthday.org in more than 193 countries.

events.title: Stephen Lawrence Day
events.summary: Established in the UK to mark the anniversary of Stephen Lawrence's death. It is a day dedicated to his memory and to fostering a fairer society for young people regardless of their background.

events.title: Jelly Bean Day
events.summary: A light-hearted day dedicated to the colourful confectionery. It is a popular date for primary schools and offices to host small charity 'guess the number of beans in the jar' competitions.

events.title: St George's Day
events.summary: The feast day of Saint George, the patron saint of England. Traditionally celebrated with the flying of the St George's Cross and community events focused on English heritage and culture.

events.title: World Book and Copyright Day
events.summary: A UNESCO initiative to promote reading, publishing, and copyright. It marks the death anniversary of William Shakespeare and Miguel de Cervantes.

events.title: English Language Day
events.summary: A UN observance day to celebrate multilingualism and cultural diversity. It is held on the date traditionally observed as both the birthday and date of death of William Shakespeare.
